description:
This table presents a hierarchical classification scheme for land cover and land use, specifically detailing categories related to wetlands and water bodies. The scheme is organised into four levels of increasing detail, with a fifth column providing a higher-level aggregation suitable for environmental reporting.

| Level 1 Category | Level 2 Category | Level 3 Category | Level 4 Category | Higher-level Aggregation |
| :--------------- | :--------------- | :--------------- | :--------------- | :----------------------- |
|                  |                  | 6.3.2 Burnt areas (except burnt forest) |                  |                          |
|                  |                  | 6.3.3 Glaciers and perpetual snow |                  |                          |
| 7. Wetland       | 7.1 Inland wetlands | 7.1.1 Inland marshes |                  | WETLAND                  |
| 7. Wetland       | 7.1 Inland wetlands | 7.1.2 Peat bogs  | 7.1.2.1 Exploited peat bogs | WETLAND                  |
| 7. Wetland       | 7.1 Inland wetlands | 7.1.2 Peat bogs  | 7.1.2.2 Unexploited peat bogs | WETLAND                  |
| 7. Wetland       | 7.2 Coastal wetlands | 7.2.1 Salt marshes |                  | MARINE INLETS AND TRANSITIONAL WATERS |
| 7. Wetland       | 7.2 Coastal wetlands | 7.2.2 Salines    |                  | MARINE INLETS AND TRANSITIONAL WATERS |
| 7. Wetland       | 7.2 Coastal wetlands | 7.2.3 Intertidal flats |                  | MARINE INLETS AND TRANSITIONAL WATERS |
| 8. Water         | 8.1 Water courses | 8.1.1 Natural & semi-Natural water courses |                  | RIVERS AND LAKES         |
| 8. Water         | 8.1 Water courses | 8.1.2 Highly modified water courses and canals |                  | RIVERS AND LAKES         |
| 8. Water         | 8.1 Water courses | 8.1.3 Seasonally connected water courses (oxbows) |                  | RIVERS AND LAKES         |
| 8. Water         | 8.2 Lakes and reservoirs | 8.2.1 Natural lakes |                  | RIVERS AND LAKES         |
| 8. Water         | 8.2 Lakes and reservoirs | 8.2.2 Reservoirs |                  | RIVERS AND LAKES         |
| 8. Water         | 8.2 Lakes and reservoirs | 8.2.3 Aquaculture ponds |                  | RIVERS AND LAKES         |
| 8. Water         | 8.2 Lakes and reservoirs | 8.2.4 Standing water bodies of extractive industrial sites |                  | RIVERS AND LAKES         |
| 8. Water         | 8.3 Transitional waters | 8.3.1 Lagoons    |                  | MARINE INLETS AND TRANSITIONAL WATERS |
| 8. Water         | 8.3 Transitional waters | 8.3.2 Estuaries  |                  | MARINE INLETS AND TRANSITIONAL WATERS |
| 8. Water         | 8.3 Transitional waters | 8.3.3 Marine inlets and fjords |                  | MARINE INLETS AND TRANSITIONAL WATERS |
| 8. Water         | 8.4 Sea and ocean | 8.4.1 Open sea   |                  | OPEN OCEAN               |
| 8. Water         | 8.4 Sea and ocean | 8.4.2 Coastal waters |                  | COASTAL                  |

The table details Level 1 categories "7. Wetland" and "8. Water," breaking them down into subclasses like "7.1 Inland wetlands" and "8.1 Water courses." These categories further specify features such as "7.1.2.1 Exploited peat bogs" and "8.1.3 Seasonally connected water courses (oxbows)." The "Higher-level Aggregation" column groups various detailed categories under broader terms like "WETLAND," "RIVERS AND LAKES," "MARINE INLETS AND TRANSITIONAL WATERS," "OPEN OCEAN," and "COASTAL," which can encompass sub-categories from different Level 1 parents. The top two rows (6.3.2 Burnt areas and 6.3.3 Glaciers) are shown without their higher-level parent categories in this excerpt.
